questions.prompt: What is the best first action before locking insulation and air-sealing scope for an Oregon project?
choices: Verify the currently adopted code cycle and jurisdiction process
Reuse last year's details without checking code updates
Order materials first and resolve scope during rough-in
questions.explanation: Code-edition control comes first; scope and sequencing should follow confirmed adopted requirements.

questions.prompt: Which Oregon statute chapter provides statewide building-code administration context for permit and inspection pathways?
questions.explanation: ORS Chapter 455 establishes Oregon's statewide building-code administration framework.

questions.prompt: Which practice best reduces concealed envelope defects before drywall?
choices: Assign penetration-sealing responsibility by trade and run staged pre-concealment checks
Let every trade decide sealing details independently on install day
Skip documentation and rely on memory during punch list
Correct missed items after turnover if complaints occur
questions.explanation: Clear accountability plus staged QA checks catches gaps before they become hidden defects.
